name: Maple Walnut Poundcake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cold, unsalted butter (cut into pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Grease an 18-inch tube pan lightly.
3. For the streusel topping:
a. In a medium bowl, stir together brown sugar, flour, butter, and cinnamon until crumbly.
b. Stir in chopped walnuts.
4. For the cake:
a. With an electric mixer on high speed, beat together butter, pancake syrup, brown sugar, vanilla, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl until blended (mixture will look curdled).
b. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition (mixture still looks curdled).
c. Gradually add flour, beating just until the batter is smooth.
5. Spoon half of the batter into the prepared pan and sprinkle with half of the streusel topping.
6. Pour in the remaining batter and spread evenly.
7. Sprinkle with the remaining streusel topping.
8. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes to 1 hour and 25 minutes, or until a pick inserted in the center comes out with moist crumbs clinging to it.
9. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 15 minutes.
10. If necessary, run a thin sharp knife around the edge of the pan to loosen the cake.
11. Place a cookie sheet over the pan and carefully invert both onto the sheet.
12. Remove the pan and place the rack over the cake, turning it streusel side up.
13. Cool completely before serving or storing wrapped airtight for up to 3 days at room temperature or freezing for up to 3 months.
